narrative: Soft US Nonfarm Payrolls and disappointing macro data dominate the weekend tape, fueling doubts over the Fed's hawkish stance and setting up a USD-bearish open. GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3350; the pair is likely to extend gains at the Tokyo open as markets price in a deeper Fed-BoE policy divergence. USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.34; while carry favors USD by +0bp, the soft US data and strong JPY options put skew (RR -1.67) would pressure the pair at Sunday's open. RBNZ's upcoming meeting on July 8 remains the primary high-impact catalyst for the week ahead.
